attorneys.name: GRETCHEN, KENNEY J

calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gretchen Kenney appeared by telephone for Kevitt Day.

The Court stated that it has the Waivers of Notice that were filed yesterday, and questioned what they were asking the Court to confirm and if there are any facts to support their request. Ms. Kenney stated that they are asking the Court to confirm the Deceased Wife's one half interest in the property, that the property was held in joint tenancy before they executed the Quitclaim Deed, that the Parties did not use an Attorney when they created the Trust and that they were not aware that the property was not in the Trust. In addition, Ms. Kenney referred to the Second Amendment, stated that the house was purchased in 1983, that they were married at that time but that she does not have the date of marriage, and that the two adult Children are their Children in common. The Petition for Spousal Property was taken under submission pending a further declaration stating the date of marriage, when the property was acquired, what community assets were used to purchase and maintain the property, and that the Children are Children in common.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chen.
